problem:
When three positive integers are divided by $12$, the remainders are $7,$ $9,$ and $10,$ respectively.

When the sum of the three integers is divided by $12$, what is the remainder?

classified_questions.question: What does it mean for the three integers to have remainders of 7, 9, and 10 when divided by 12?
classified_questions.label: f

classified_questions.question: Can you explain why we can represent the three integers as congruences modulo 12?
classified_questions.label: r

classified_questions.question: When we add the three congruences together, what do we get?
classified_questions.label: b

classified_questions.question: How can we simplify the sum of the congruences using modular arithmetic?
classified_questions.label: b

classified_questions.question: What is the remainder when 26 is divided by 12?
classified_questions.label: b

classified_questions.question: How does this result help us determine the remainder when the sum of the three integers is divided by 12?
classified_questions.label: b
